source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the diagram, each of the squares touches adjacent squares at its corners and the line $G H$ along one of its edges. The line $G H$ is $24 \mathrm{~cm}$ long. What is the total perimeter, in centimetres, of all the squares? <image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly begins with 'In the diagram,' indicating the geometric configuration is needed.
The description references spatial/visual relations: squares touching at corners and line GH lying along one edge of each square.
An image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual dependency is supported by the registry.
multi_step: Solving likely requires reading the diagram structure, relating GH=24 cm to the side lengths of multiple squares, then aggregating to a total perimeter.
This is not a direct lookup; it involves at least visual interpretation plus a perimeter computation.
Answer type is numeric with no multiple-choice options, suggesting constructed reasoning rather than answer selection.
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='96', which is easy to compare against a final computed result.
The geometry setup appears deterministic rather than open-ended, so a derived perimeter can be checked.
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true.

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues
high image_dependency_score is supported by an existing image asset, so the core visual semantics appear recoverable
high verifiability_score with a numeric answer makes the sample easy to check once solved
although the text alone does not fully specify the visual configuration, the required diagram is available, so this is not a core-semantic gap
